title: Fermented Portuguese Rhubarb Galette
description: A light and silky dessert featuring fermented rhubarb and infused oil, inspired by Portuguese cuisine.

ingredients.name: Spices
ingredients.notes: 2 teaspoons, ground cinnamon and ground ginger

instructions: Combine yeast, sugar, and warm water in a bowl, and let it sit for 5 minutes to activate.
Add flour, salt, and spices to the bowl, and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 2 hours, or until doubled in size.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch, and transfer it to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Toss the rhubarb with sugar, and spread it evenly over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the dough over the rhubarb, and brush the infused oil over the dough.
Bake the galette in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for 30 minutes, or until the dough is golden brown and the rhubarb is tender.
Let the galette cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes, before slicing and serving.

To make the infused oil, combine 1 cup of olive oil with 1/4 cup of chopped fresh rosemary and 2 cloves of minced garlic in a saucepan, and heat it over low heat for 30 minutes, then strain and cool the oil.
